Title: Avoid Lua "a and b or default" for booleans
Rule: For boolean values, do not use `a and b or default`; use explicit conditionals or boolean-safe expressions.

Title: Don't bake Arabic shaping into config strings at load time
Rule: Keep config metadata as raw strings and apply shaping/RTL at render time (or guarantee TOC load order for shaping helpers).

Title: Re-parent shared overlay elements when switching parent frames
Rule: If an overlay is reused across multiple parent frames, call `SetParent(currentParent)` each time you position/apply it.

Title: Tooltip placeholder functions must actually substitute values
Rule: When translation data contains `{1}`, `{2}`, etc., implement extraction from the original and substitute into the translation before RTL processing.

Title: printf-style format tokens must be protected before RTL reversal
Rule: In `HandleWoWSpecialCodes`, protect printf tokens (standard + positional) before running RTL reversal.

Title: Values substituted before RTL reversal must be marked for protection
Rule: When substituting values into text that will be RTL-reversed, wrap values with markers so the special-code protector preserves them.

Title: Number extraction patterns must require at least one digit
Rule: Use number patterns that require a digit (e.g. `%-?%d[%d,]*%.?%d*`), not patterns that match commas alone.

Title: RTL justification must check for actual Arabic content, not just locale
Rule: Apply RTL justification only when the tooltip actually contains Arabic characters, not merely when the addon is in Arabic mode.

Title: Changelog dates must be data, not runtime time()
Rule: Store historical changelog dates as stable data strings (or parsed stable timestamps), never `date()`/`time()` at load/build time.

Title: UTF-8 char-byte helpers must never return nil
Rule: Ensure all paths in UTF-8 byte-length helpers return a number; for invalid/unexpected bytes return 1.

Title: Persian/Urdu shaping must use Presentation Forms-A for extended letters
Rule: For Persian/Urdu extended letters, map to the correct Presentation Forms-A (FB50–FDFF) code points, not Forms-B (FE70–FEFF).
